chapter.label: Tale 189
chapter.title: The life of a woman who REMAINED

paragraphs.text: FAITHFUL TO HER HUSBAND, A MERCHANT, and his wife, he sighed to himself and said: ‘Look what a situation AND HOW GOD HELPED THEM BOTH . they are in—yet they would not surrender their honour* <cleutheria> neither for money, or to be set free. They held chastity to We came too the hospice of the Fathers at Ascalon and there be of more worth than all riches and they despised all the things of Eusebios the priest said to us: this life. And what shall I do, wretcl: that I am, who have never There was a merchant of our city who sei sail and fost all his own even thought about the question of whether there is a God—and on goods and everything else he was carrying at sea. He alone was that account, I am responsible for so many murders. He called saved. When he came back he was seized by bis creditors and them over and through the window of <the cell> where he lay, he thrown into prison. Everything in his housc was confiscated. There said to them: ‘T was a robber and mauy are the evil deeds and the was nothing left to him except what he and his wile stood up in. murders I have committed. And for that reason, when the governor Although she was in great distress and anxiety, she made it a rule comes and [ appear before him, I shall die as a murderer. Yet when at least to feed her husband with bread. One day, as she was sitting 1 saw your chastity, I was moved with compassion for you, Go to eating with her husband in prison, a person of note came in to such-and-such a place by the city-wall; dig there, and take the distribute some comforts to the inmates. He took her robber to be brought out and beheaded. The day afterwards, the aside and said to her: “Wha is the matter? He debt, will you sleep with me tonight?” She, who was very beautiful said to her: ‘Do what you think best’. She got a small mattock and and very pure-minded, said to him: ‘My lord, I have heard the in the evening she went and dug at the spot he had mentioned. She Apostle <Paul; 2 Co 7:1-7> say that a wife does not have authority found a covered pot with gold in it. She used it very prudently, over her own body: her husband has. He was a wise man who loved his debts>. Then she was also able to get her husband out.
